MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank N.A. seeks summary judgment against defendant Feng Huang. This case arises out of Wells Fargo's issuance of a credit card to Huang. Wells Fargo brings a cause of action for breach of contract and a cause of action for common count open book account money due. Huang has not opposed the motion. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants summary judgment to Wells Fargo.

"A party may move for summary judgment in an action or proceeding if it is contended that the action has no merit or that there is no defense to the action or proceeding." (CCP 437c(a)(1).) "[T]he party moving for summary judgment bears an initial burden of production to make a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any triable issue of material fact; if he carries his burden of production, he causes a shift, and the opposing party is then subjected to a burden of production of his own to make a prima facie showing of the existence of a triable issue of material fact." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) "[I]f a plaintiff who would bear the burden of proof by a preponderance of evidence at trial moves for summary judgment, he must present evidence that would require a reasonable trier of fact to find any underlying material fact more likely than not-otherwise, he would not be entitled to judgment as a matter of law . . . ." (Id. at p. 851.)

Wells Fargo has satisfied its burden. On the common count open book account claim, its evidence shows that Huang opened a credit card account, accepted terms of service creating a contract with Wells Fargo, and accrued a balance. (Dennis Dec. paras. 6-9 & Exs. A & B.) Its evidence shows that Wells Fargo maintained a record of the account and periodically mailed statements to Huang (Dennis Dec. para. 10 & Ex. C), and that Huang owed a balance of $8,029.19 when he stopped payments on the account (Dennis Dec. para. 12). On the same evidence, Wells Fargo has satisfied its burden as to the breach of contract claim: the parties formed a contract whereby Wells Fargo issued credit to Huang and Huang agreed to repay it; Wells Fargo substantially performed its obligations under the contract; Huang breached the contract by failing to do something it required him to do (repay his balance), and Wells Fargo was harmed by his nonperformance.

Proposed order Granting Motion for Summary Judgment is submitted. The court grants summary judgment to Wells Fargo. Wells Fargo shall submit a proposed judgment.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
